key_points: All laser radiation protocols tested (Nd:YAG, Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG) and antimicrobial photodynamic therapy (aPDT) demonstrated effective bactericidal effects in experimentally infected root canals.
The Er:YAG laser combined with NaClO/NS/DW irrigation protocol showed the most effective bactericidal results among the tested protocols.
The study evaluated bactericidal efficacy against experimentally infected root canals, likely using Enterococcus faecalis as the test organism.
Antimicrobial photodynamic therapy (aPDT) was included as a comparison group alongside laser irradiation protocols.
The combination of Er:YAG laser with sodium hypochlorite (NaClO), normal saline (NS), and distilled water (DW) was proposed as an ideal protocol for root canal disinfection during endodontic therapy.

outcomes.measured: Bactericidal effect in infected root canals
outcomes.result: All laser protocols (Nd:YAG, Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG) and aPDT showed effective bactericidal effects; Er:YAG/NaClO/NS/DW protocol showed the best results
outcomes.clinical_significance: Er:YAG combined with NaClO/NS/DW proposed as ideal protocol for root canal disinfection

notes: Full text not available; details on specific laser parameters, sample size, and statistical analysis are based on abstract only. The irrigants used (NaClO = sodium hypochlorite, NS = normal saline, DW = distilled water) were combined with laser irradiation.

population.sample_type: Ex vivo/in vitro infected root canal specimens

superiority_claim: El autor afirma que 'Er:YAG/NaClO/NS/DW seems to be an ideal protocol for root canal disinfection during endodontic therapy', sugiriendo superioridad de este protocolo sobre los demás evaluados.
source_citation: Cheng, X., Guan, S.-M., Lu, H., Zhao, C., Chen, X.-X., Li, N., Bai, Q., Tian, Y., & Yu, Q. (2012). Evaluation of the bactericidal effect of Nd:YAG, Er:YAG, Er,Cr:YSGG laser radiation, and antimicrobial photodynamic therapy (aPDT) in experimentally infected root canals. Lasers in Surgery and Medicine. https://doi.org/10.1002/lsm.22092
uncertainties: Full text not available; specific laser parameters (power, energy, pulse duration, frequency, spot size) not extractable from abstract alone.
Sample size (n) and statistical analysis details not reported in abstract.
Exact bacterial species used for experimental infection not confirmed in abstract (likely Enterococcus faecalis based on keywords).
aPDT photosensitizer type and light source wavelength not specified.
Study type (in vitro vs ex vivo) not explicitly stated; classified as in_vitro based on context.
Conflict of interest and funding information not available.
